title: Binary for buildah
body: I need binary for buildah. So any pointers about it? I see buildah does not provides it.

comments.body: Would a container image suffice?  If so quay.io/buildah:stable, quay.io/buildah:upstream, quay.io/buildah:stable
What distribution do you want it on?

I use Ubuntu(Bionic Beaver). I am new in this container business, so if binaries are available at quay.io, kindly give me some pointers regarding it after pulling the image.
Thank you.
You can install Buildah on Ubuntu via a ppa as explained in the docs: https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/install.md#ubuntu
So I think I will get binary in usr/local/bin after installation! And what is the case with quay.io one?

@theexplorist The one on quay.io is buildah within a container image, so you could run this buildah via Podman or Docker.
Most likely the one from the PPA is what you were after.
@rhatdan  Is it possible to download and extract the tar file and extract the binary by compiling it? Is it necessary to install certain packages?
@theexplorist, yes. You can find the details in the docs: https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/install.md#building-from-scratch
@vrothberg  the ppa installs the latest dev versions, are those dev versions stable? If not I think ppa should only provide the stable buildah and runc versions.
@lsm5 will know what's going on with the Ubuntu packages.

@theexplorist the ppa will currently get you buildah 1.10.1-1\~ubuntu18.04\~ppa1 which is not dev. The only dev versions are for cri-o which are built using the release-1.x branches which AFAIK are considered stable for use.
